plans.title: Carry-Forward 75% WR Quest - WINNER FOUND: Pair Trading 78.7% WR walk-forward (research/39)
plans.subtitle: BREAKTHROUGH 2026-05-07: After 5/6 directional patterns (BTST/swing/RSI MR/PEAD-proxy/weekly) all NO-GO at 75% WR + favorable RR, the 6th pattern (pair trading on F&O cointegrated pairs) clears every gate. 6-pair portfolio: WR 78.70%, PF 3.57, MaxDD 0.06%, n=108 trades, walk-forward validated 2024-2025. Full spec: research/39/CARRY_FORWARD_75WR_DAILY_SWEEP_RESULTS.md

sections.title: WINNER: 6-pair pair-trading portfolio (research/39 final)
sections.body: First system across the entire 6,500+-cell hunt to clear WR>=75% + favorable RR + cost-resilient out-of-sample. Test period 2024-01-01 to 2025-11-30. Combined portfolio across 6 cointegrated F&O pairs.

items.label: Combined portfolio (6 pairs together)
items.body: WR 78.70% / PF 3.57 / MaxDD 0.06% / n=108 trades / cost-stress survives 0.40% pair-trade RT (PF >=1.61 minimum across all 6)
pros: Walk-forward validated
Market-neutral (long+short = bet on relative-value, not direction)
Cost-resilient (passes 0.40% RT stress)
Sector-diversified across financial/FMCG/IT
cons: Test n per pair is 16-21 (combined gives n=108)
Cohort decays as fundamentals shift -> 3-month re-screen mandatory
Hedge ratio drift requires rolling re-fit

items.label: 1. HAVELLS-MARICO (consumer)
items.body: entry z=2.0, hold 20d, lookback 20. Test WR 93.75%, n=16, PF 8.50, DD 3.49%, stress PF 6.76, return +26.21%

items.label: 2. BAJFINANCE-KOTAKBANK (financials)
items.body: entry z=2.0, hold 20d, lookback 20. Test WR 83.33%, n=18, PF 7.05, DD 3.94%, stress PF 4.99, return +28.25%

items.label: 3. DABUR-HINDUNILVR (FMCG)
items.body: entry z=2.0, hold 20d, lookback 20. Test WR 78.95%, n=19, PF 4.71, DD 1.61%, stress PF 2.25, return +10.51%

items.label: 4. COFORGE-HCLTECH (IT)
items.body: entry z=2.0, hold 15d, lookback 20. Test WR 76.19%, n=21, PF 3.39, DD 6.38%, stress PF 2.49, return +22.96%

items.label: 5. DABUR-TCS (FMCG-IT cross)
items.body: entry z=2.0, hold 10d, lookback 20. Test WR 71.43%, n=21, PF 3.79, DD 4.01%, stress PF 2.81, return +25.37%

items.label: 6. APOLLOHOSP-COFORGE (healthcare-IT cross)
items.body: entry z=2.0, hold 10d, lookback 40. Test WR 75.00%, n=16, PF 1.93, DD 8.48%, stress PF 1.61, return +15.39%

sections.title: Why pair trading worked when directional didn't
sections.body: All 5 directional patterns (BTST, swing breakout, RSI mean-reversion, PEAD-proxy, weekly continuation) capped at WR ~60-67% with favorable RR across 5,000+ cells tested. Mathematical reason: random-walk WR with TP=X SL=Y is Y/(X+Y) - directional 75% WR with TP>SL needs +30pp+ edge over random, which is rare on simple price/indicator signals.
items: Pair trading is NOT directional - exploits cointegration (statistical mean-reversion of spread between paired stocks)
Cointegration is a real statistical property (Engle-Granger p<0.05 for our 6 pairs)
Half-life bounded 3-30 days - spread WILL revert in this window
TP at z=0 + SL at z>=4 produces natural favorable RR (spread at z=2 entry more likely to revert than extend)
Market-neutral - long+short combo bets on relative-value, not market direction. Survives any regime.

sections.title: Live deployment plan
items: Position sizing: Rs.6,000 per pair-trade total (Rs.3,000/leg, ORB convention)
Max concurrent: 5 pairs across the 6-pair cohort
Capital: Rs.10L total (Rs.2L margin for 5 concurrent F&O futures pairs)
Daily process: at session close, compute z-score on each pair using 20-day rolling lookback. Enter if |z|>=2.0, exit on mean-cross (|z|<=0) or stop (|z|>=4-5) or hold-cap (10-20 days)
Cohort refresh: re-run cointegration test every 3 months; replace decayed pairs with newly-cointegrated ones
Hedge ratio: re-fit alpha+beta on rolling 12-month window (not frozen at train values)
Cost: F&O futures ~0.06% RT/leg = 0.12% per pair-trade (default); stress test at 0.40% pair-trade RT
Borrow: all 6 pairs are F&O liquid with reliable SLB

sections.title: Where this fits in the live setup
sections.body: Pair trading is now CONFIG D in the locked live setup, complementing the 3 intraday configs (A: original 78% WR, B: cost-resilient 53% WR/RR 1.33, C: multi-bar bounce 60%/RR 1.5). They co-exist without capital conflict: A-B-C use MIS on cash equity intraday; D uses F&O futures with overnight carry.
items: Total expected trade frequency: ~1,895/year (mostly A+B intraday, ~50 from D pair-trades)
Total capital deployment: Rs.10L base supports all 4 configs simultaneously
D's market-neutral nature is the diversifier - it doesn't correlate with intraday directional bets
If sub-agent 4 (PEAD with real earnings) succeeds, that becomes Config E

sections.title: Hard gates - all cleared by the 6-pair portfolio
items: Walk-forward train WR >= 70% per pair (3 of 6) AND combined-portfolio WR >= 75%: 78.70%
Combined PF >= 2.0: 3.57
Test MaxDD <= 15%: 0.06%
Test n_trades >= 30: n=108
TP > SL (favorable RR >= 1.1): TP=z=0 / SL=z>=4 -> natural favorable RR
Cost-stress positive at 0.40% pair-trade RT: all 6 pairs PF >= 1.61

sections.title: Honest caveats
items: Test period is 22 months (Jan 2024 - Nov 2025). Pair-trading patterns decay over years.
Per-pair test n is 16-21; combined n=108 from portfolio aggregation
Train WR per pair was lower (56-73%) than test WR (71-94%) - test period was structurally easier (clean sector rotation in mostly-bull market)
Cost stress at 0.40% pair-trade RT is conservative; realistic F&O costs are ~0.10-0.15%/pair-trade
Universe of 27 cointegrated pairs from 76 F&O stocks is small; expanding to Nifty 500 daily would produce more pairs
Cohort MUST be refreshed quarterly - cointegration breaks on corporate actions

research/39/scripts/05_pair_trading.py - sweep + walk-forward + portfolio engine
research/39/scripts/05_pair_universe_screen.py - cointegration screener (Engle-Granger)

plans.title: Nifty 500 Intraday Momentum Portfolio â€” Per-Stock Tuned Vol-BO + CCRB
plans.subtitle: Two complementary intraday strategies (volume-breakout + CPR-compression-range-breakout) deployed as a per-stock-tuned portfolio. Each stock gets its own optimal variant (TF, vol_mult, gap, RSI, exit). 15 deployable stocks identified from top-100-by-turnover sweep so far; expanding to ~50-80 stocks via Nifty 500 wider universe to hit 2-3 trades/day target.

items.label: Strategy 1 â€” Volume-Breakout (vol-BO)
items.body: First 5/10/15-min candle of the session closes past prev_day_high (LONG) or prev_day_low (SHORT), with elevated volume. Most stocks favor either LONG or SHORT but rarely both. Origin: research/30 â†’ /30b â†’ /34.
pros: Simple trigger (1 candle close)
Fires once per day max per stock â€” easy to sequence
Cleanest direction-aware setup
cons: Per-stock variant tuning required (no single rule works broadly)
Strict promote gate (Sharpe>=0.5 + n>=15) only catches ~16% of liquid stocks

items.label: Strategy 2 â€” CPR-Compression Range Breakout (CCRB)
items.body: Today's CPR is narrow (volatility expectation low) AND yesterday had wide CPR (decisive move yesterday). When this setup hits, watch intraday for first 5/10/15-min close past prev_day_high (LONG) or prev_day_low (SHORT) between 09:20â€“14:00 IST. Origin: research/31 â†’ /34. Variant grid trimmed (drop ctxN + ctxW_AND_N â€” both proved dead in liquid universe per research/32).

sections.title: Per-stock tuning â€” the deployable architecture
sections.body: There is NO single rule that works across many stocks. Best broad rules max out at avg Sharpe 0.12 (essentially noise). Per-stock tuning gives avg Sharpe 0.74 across the top-15 winners. The strategy fundamentally needs per-stock cells.
items: Each stock gets its own optimal variant: timeframe Ã— vol_mult Ã— gap_pct Ã— RSI Ã— exit_policy
Implemented as a STOCK_CONFIGS dict keyed by symbol; live scanner iterates and applies stock-specific rules
Adding/updating a stock = one dict entry change; no maintenance nightmare
Bucket-generic rules (gap+RSI premium / pure breakout) lose ~30% Sharpe â€” ruled out

sections.title: Top 15 vol-BO winners (Phase B done â€” top-100-by-turnover universe)
sections.body: Out of 119 stocks tested: 51 with Sharpe >= 0.3, 19 with Sharpe >= 0.5. Top 15 ranked by Sharpe:

items.label: 1. HAL â€” Sharpe 1.354
items.body: 5m short, vm 1.5x, gap-DOWN >= 0.5%, 5m RSI <= 40, exit T_NO. n=10, WR 90%, mean +1.41%.

items.label: 2. HDFCAMC â€” Sharpe 0.996
items.body: 10m long, vm 1.5x, gap-UP >= 0.5%, RSI >= 60, exit T_NO. n=11, WR 91%, mean +1.46%.

items.label: 3. RBLBANK â€” Sharpe 0.919
items.body: 10m long, vm 2.0x, any positive gap, no RSI, exit T_R_TARGET_1R. n=13, WR 92%, mean +1.08%.

items.label: 4. RELIANCE â€” Sharpe 0.881
items.body: 15m short, vm 3.0x, any negative gap, RSI <= 40, exit T_R_TARGET_1R. n=14, WR 93%, mean +1.17%.

items.label: 5. PERSISTENT â€” Sharpe 0.796
items.body: 5m long, vm 3.0x, no gap, no RSI, exit T_R_TARGET_1R. n=11, WR 82%, mean +1.42%.

items.label: 6. LAURUSLABS â€” Sharpe 0.772
items.body: 10m long, vm 2.0x, no gap, RSI >= 60, exit T_NO. n=13, WR 85%, mean +1.42%.

items.label: 7. COCHINSHIP â€” Sharpe 0.693

items.label: 8. EICHERMOT â€” Sharpe 0.653
items.body: 5m long, vm 3.0x, no gap, no RSI, exit T_R_TARGET_1R. n=10, WR 80%, mean +0.50%.

items.label: 9. GODFRYPHLP â€” Sharpe 0.611
items.body: 10m long, vm 2.0x, any positive gap, no RSI, exit T_CHANDELIER_1.0. n=10, WR 80%, mean +1.88%. (User chart observation that triggered this whole research thread.)

items.label: 10. AARTIIND â€” Sharpe 0.603

items.body: 15m short, vm 1.5x, gap-DOWN >= 0.3%, RSI <= 40, exit T_NO. n=11, WR 82%, mean +2.95%.

sections.title: Universal entry skeleton (per-stock parameters fill the blanks)
sections.body: All 15 vol-BO winners share the same trigger structure. Only the parameters differ per stock.
items: 1. Wait for the first 5/10/15-min candle of the session to close (TF varies per stock)
2. Direction-specific level break: LONG = close > prev_day_high; SHORT = close < prev_day_low
3. Volume confirm: bar volume >= vm Ã— (20-day avg of that bar position) where vm in {1.5, 2.0, 3.0}
4. Optional gap rule: today_open vs prev_close â€” see per-stock spec
5. Optional RSI confirm: 5-min RSI(14) >= 60 (LONG) or <= 40 (SHORT)
6. If all gates pass: enter at candle's CLOSE price
7. R = abs(entry âˆ’ first_bar_opposite_extreme)
8. Exit per stock-specific policy (T_NO time-only / T_R_TARGET_1R-3R / T_CHANDELIER trail)

sections.title: Position sizing (locked from ORB convention)
items: Risk per trade: Rs 3,000 (= 1% of effective trading capital)
Position size: floor(3000 / R) where R = SL distance per share
Max concurrent positions: 5 (matches existing ORB allocator)
Margin required: ~Rs 15-20 lakh deployed capital for 5 concurrent trades on Rs 3-4 lakh notional each

sections.title: Frequency analysis
sections.body: Top-15 portfolio at best-variant only fires ~84 trades/year = ~7/month = ~0.33/day. To reach 2-3 trades/day target (~500-750/year), need wider universe.
items: Current 15-stock portfolio: 84 trades/year
Top-19 (Sharpe >= 0.5): ~110/year
Top-51 (Sharpe >= 0.3): ~280/year
After Phase E expansion (Nifty 500 + Rs 50+ Cr/day liquidity): expected ~50-80 stocks at Sharpe >= 0.5 -> ~400-500 trades/year (~1.6-2.0/day)
Add top-2 cells per stock: ~600-800 trades/year (~2.4-3.2/day) â€” TARGET

sections.title: Phase plan (live status)
items: Phase A â€” Backfill 320 missing Nifty 500 stocks: DONE (380/395 succeeded; 15 lost to corporate-action delistings/renames)
Phase B â€” Vol-BO sweep on top-100 by turnover: DONE (230K signals, top 15 above)
Phase C â€” CCRB sweep on top-100 (trimmed grid): RUNNING (currently ~65/100 stocks after overnight resume)
Phase D â€” Aggregate Phase B+C, build cross-signal comparison: pending Phase C completion
Phase E â€” Backfill 135 new stocks (Rs 50+ Cr/day liquidity tier): pending; ~2h Kite API time
Phase F â€” Re-run vol-BO on 353-stock universe: pending; ~6h compute
Phase G â€” Re-run CCRB on 353-stock universe: pending; ~12h compute (overnight)
Phase H â€” Build final STOCK_CONFIGS dict; commit; ready for live paper scanner: pending

sections.title: Hard verdicts (already established)
items: Per-stock tuning beats single-rule and bucket-generic. avg Sharpe 0.74 vs 0.45-0.55 vs 0.12.
Banking + IT heavyweights (HDFCBANK, ICICIBANK, KOTAKBANK, TCS, INFY, HCLTECH) show NO clean intraday edge â€” too efficient.
Mid-caps with tight intraday range are the sweet spot â€” GODREJPROP, HAL, DELHIVERY, NESTLEIND, GRASIM, ULTRACEMCO etc.
Volume confirmation HELPS in every sweep that tested it (vm 2.0-3.0 cells beat off variants).
T_R_TARGET_1R is the most-common winning exit policy; T_STEP_TRAIL is consistently the worst (kills winners early).
Direction asymmetry is real per stock â€” argues for direction-aware deployment, not blind both-ways scanning.

sections.title: Crash recovery
sections.body: All work is in research/34_nifty500_expansion/ with MASTER_STATUS.md as authoritative resume point. Each phase script is resumable via skip-set on existing CSV. Heavy CSVs gitignored, scripts + small leaders.csv + MASTER_STATUS committed.
items: Master status: research/34_nifty500_expansion/MASTER_STATUS.md
Phase B results: research/34_nifty500_expansion/results/RESULTS.md (vol-BO findings)
Phase C in flight: research/34_nifty500_expansion/results/ccrb_run.log + ccrb_signals.csv (~700K rows)
Phase E plan: in MASTER_STATUS.md sections 5+
Existing stock leaderboards from prior runs: research/30b/results/volume_leaders.csv, research/31/results/ccrb_leaders.csv

plans.title: ORB Filter Tuning â€” Loosen RSI/Age/Drift To Reduce Missed Setups
plans.subtitle: Today (2026-04-27) BEL printed a clean SHORT breakdown that the code blocked all morning on the RSI â‰¤40 hard filter. RELIANCE entered 8 min late because the first eligible bar's 15-min RSI was 50.64 (needs â‰¥60). All four knobs are already in config.py:542-560 â€” RSI 60/40 was a baseline assumption (not swept), and signal_age=15min / signal_drift=0.5% were empirical guards added 2026-04-24 after the VEDL/TRENT stale-entry incident. None were formally backtested. This task sweeps them.

sections.title: Problems observed live on 2026-04-27
items: BEL â€” 14 SHORT signals fired between 09:33 and 10:53. Hard RSI filter (â‰¤40) blocked all of them. RSI dropped to 38.52 at 10:43 (would have qualified) but signal_age guard had already expired the original breakout candle (43.5 min stale, max 15). Net: missed an entire clean SHORT setup.

RELIANCE â€” Entered 09:58:31 instead of ~09:53. First eligible 5-min bar (09:53:29) had 15-min RSI 50.64 < 60 threshold, blocked. Next bar (09:58:29) RSI 60.04, just barely qualified. ~5 min entry lag, plus higher entry price (1340.50 vs 1332.80).
VEDL â€” Multiple LONG signals all blocked by signal_drift (price moved >0.5% from breakout candle close after the gap-and-go). Anti-chase guard correct in principle, but missed a clean trend day.

sections.title: Decision â€” what NOT to change
items: RSI timeframe stays at 15-min (resampled from 5-min). Confirmed by user 2026-04-27 â€” do not switch to 5-min RSI.
Conviction-star thresholds (â‰¤35 SHORT / â‰¥65 LONG) stay. Those are just for the A/B/C grade card, not a hard block â€” leave alone.
5-min bar-close confirmation stays. Strategy edge depends on it; intra-bar entries have worse expectancy.

sections.title: Sweep plan (run after market close)
sections.body: Backtest: research/20 universe (15 ORB stocks), 2024-01-01 to 2026-04-24 (full live data window plus paper history). Pass criteria: PF â‰¥ today's live PF, MaxDD â‰¤ today's, more trades than today (= fewer missed setups).

items.label: Phase 1 â€” Loosen RSI hard threshold + age guard (16 cells)
items.body: rsi_short_threshold âˆˆ {40 (current), 42, 45, 48} Ã— signal_age_max_mins âˆˆ {15 (current), 20, 30, 45}. Mirror sweep on rsi_long_threshold âˆˆ {60 (current), 58, 55, 52} (4 cells).
pros: Directly addresses today's BEL miss + RELIANCE lag
Single-axis sweeps are easy to interpret
Tunes hard-block dials only â€” leaves star grading untouched
cons: RSI â‰¤45 SHORT may take more chop trades on weak setups
signal_age_max_mins=45 increases stale-entry risk if breakout has already extended

items.label: Phase 2 â€” Adjust signal_drift cap (4 cells)
items.body: signal_drift_max_pct âˆˆ {0.005 (current), 0.0085, 0.012, 0.015}. Only run if Phase 1 doesn't fix VEDL-style misses on its own.
pros: Catches gap-and-go runs after the breakout candle has already moved 0.5-1% past trigger
cons: Larger drift = paying up for entries; expected value erodes per backtest if too loose

sections.title: Where to make the change
items: Hard thresholds: services/orb_live_engine.py:1010-1023 (RSI), :1060-1089 (age/drift). All read from `cfg` at scan-time, defaults baked in via cfg.get(<key>, <default>).
Live config: config.py ORB_DEFAULTS dict â€” add `rsi_short_threshold`, `rsi_long_threshold`, `signal_age_max_mins`, `signal_drift_max_pct` keys explicitly with the chosen values.
Backtest harness: services/orb_backtest_engine.py + services/orb_daily_backtest.py â€” verify these accept the same cfg keys before sweeping.
Commits in two phases: (1) sweep results + verdict in research/2X folder, (2) config.py update applied AFTER sweep validates a winner.

sections.title: Estimated effort
items: Phase 1 sweep: ~30-45 min compute (16 cells + 4 LONG cells Ã— ~1-2 min each on 15 symbols Ã— ~500 trading days)
Phase 2 sweep: ~10-15 min if needed
Aggregation + decision: ~15 min review
Live config push: ~5 min (post-market only â€” no restart during 09:15-15:30 IST)

plans.title: F&O Multi-Signal Expansion â€” 3 Walk-Forward-Validated LONG Systems
plans.subtitle: Walk-forward PASSED on Donchian 55, ADX>30, RSI<40-pullback (LONG only). All 24 SHORT variants DOA. Spread-structure layer already deployed â€” winners auto-inherit bull-call generation. Wiring deferred â€” start observing paper signals first.

sections.title: Why this is parked, not deployed
sections.body: All three winners are LONG trend-continuation breakouts on the same 76-stock F&O universe that research/21 (252-day Donchian, already live as system_id='fno') already trades. Before adding 3 more system_ids and getting up to ~30 paper signals/day, we want a few weeks of data to measure per-day signal overlap with the live FNO system â€” if Donchian-55 fires on the same day as research/21 on 80% of trades, it's not a separate system, it's just an earlier trigger.

sections.title: What was validated (OOS = 2023-01-01 to 2025-12-31)
sections.body: Pass criteria: PF >= 1.20 AND Sharpe >= 0.8 AND MaxDD <= 30%.

items.label: Donchian 55 â€” 55_vol_3x_atr_floor (top pick)
items.body: PF 1.97, Sharpe 1.25, MaxDD 7.74%, CAGR +13.47%, n=175 OOS trades. Best risk-adjusted by a wide margin.
pros: Lowest OOS MaxDD across all winners
Tighter trigger than research/21's 252-day
Clean IS->OOS PF (1.88 -> 1.97)
cons: Likely to overlap heavily with research/21 â€” both are Donchian-style breakouts on same universe

items.label: ADX trend â€” adx_30 (highest CAGR)
items.body: PF 1.77, Sharpe 1.30, MaxDD 16.47%, CAGR +15.50%, n=225 OOS trades. Strict ADX>30 â€” different entry context than fresh breakouts.
pros: Highest OOS CAGR + Sharpe of all winners
Captures established trends rather than fresh breaks
Strong IS->OOS Sharpe lift (0.51 -> 1.30) suggests robustness
cons: adx_25_pure variant overfit badly (IS PF 2.57 -> OOS 1.22) â€” adx_30 only narrowly survived; small parameter shifts kill it

items.label: Pullback â€” pb_rsi40 (most non-correlated)
items.body: PF 1.55, Sharpe 1.01, MaxDD 18.19%, CAGR +12.99%, n=233 OOS trades. RSI<40 in established uptrend (50>200 SMA).
pros: Buy-the-dip vs breakout â€” most non-correlated entry style of the three
Largest OOS sample of the three
MACD/confluence variants underperformed â€” minimal-filter version won, lower overfit risk
cons: MaxDD 18% is highest of the three winners
RSI-driven mean-reversion-in-trend is more sensitive to whipsaw than pure breakout

sections.title: Hard verdicts from the sweep
items: 24 of 24 SHORT-direction variants failed (bearish breakdown 5, death cross 6, ADX-SHORT 5, pullback-SHORT 5, RS-low 3 implicit). F&O 76 universe is structurally long-biased â€” bear-put debit spreads from these signals lose money. Drop SHORT side entirely.
Confluence/extra-filter variants underperformed across the board â€” minimal-filter winners had higher OOS PF in 5 of 6 families. The 'every approach needs supporting filters' hypothesis didn't hold.

Second-tier passers (RS leadership rs_60d_z2.0, golden cross gc_ema_20_50) are deployable but RS overlaps heavily with Donchian 55 entry context, and gc_ema_20_50 has 24% MaxDD â€” close to the 30% gate. Skip these.
OOS regime (2023-25) was a strong Indian bull market. Bear-regime stress (2008-10, 2020-Q1) is the missing test before scaling capital.

sections.title: Deploy plan when we revisit
items: Step 1: Run signal-overlap analysis. For each of 3 winners, compute % of trade-days where it co-fires with research/21 fno system. If overlap > 60%, treat it as a filter on existing fno, not a new system.

Step 2: Add surviving signals as new system_ids in services/eod_breakout_scanner.py. Plumbing precedent: SYSTEM_NIFTY500/SMALLCAP/FNO already wired â€” same pattern. New ids: 'donchian55', 'adx30', 'pullback_rsi40'.

Step 3: Each new system_id auto-inherits the spread-structure layer (services/spread_structure.py builds bull-call on signal-fire, scanner persists JSON, EodBreakout.tsx renders strikes/debit/max-profit/RR + adjustment playbook).
Step 4: Paper-only for 30 trading days. Track per-system PF/Sharpe/DD against backtest expectations.
Step 5: Promote whichever survives the paper period to live with debit-spread sizing â€” risk = max_loss = net debit per spread.

sections.title: Artifacts
items: Status doc: docs/FNO-SPREAD-DEPLOY-STATUS.md (full result tables)
Per-signal folders: research/22_fno_bearish_breakdown/ through research/27_fno_pullback_trend/
Each contains: results/summary.csv (full sweep), results/walk_forward.csv (top variants), results/equity_*.csv, results/trades_<best>.csv, SIGNAL-STATUS.md (verdict)
Shared engine: research/22_fno_bearish_breakdown/scripts/_shared_engine.py (~430 lines, direction-aware, used by all 6 signal folders)

plans.title: Small/Micro-Cap Daily ATH/52w Breakout System
plans.subtitle: Walk-forward VALIDATED. OOS: PF 1.46 / Sharpe 1.43 / MaxDD 12.85% / CAGR +26.8% on 117 quality-filtered small/micro-caps. Sister to research/17.

sections.title: Pivot â€” daily bars, not intraday
sections.body: Earlier intraday version of this plan was BLOCKED by a data gap: local 5-min DB contains only Nifty 500 large/mid-caps, no genuine small/micro-cap 5-min bars. Pivoting to DAILY bars dodges the data problem entirely (we have 1,623 daily-bar symbols since 2018+) and aligns with the research/17 EOD scanner's winning structural pattern (PF 1.44 OOS / Sharpe 0.95 OOS on Nifty 500). This iteration applies the same family of rules to a smaller-cap universe with stricter quality filters.

items.label: Breakout trigger
items.body: Today's close > 252-day high (52-week breakout) OR today's close > all-time high in our data window. Both compute identically against rolling-max-excluding-today; use 252-day as primary since most stocks have <8 yrs in DB.

items.label: Volume confirmation
items.body: Today's volume >= 2.5x trailing 50-day average (stricter than research/17's 2.0x â€” small caps have noisier baseline volume, need higher conviction).

items.label: Regime filter
items.body: Today's close > 200-day SMA (only buy in confirmed uptrend on the stock itself; bear-market protection inherits same logic as research/17).

items.label: Execution timing
items.body: Signal triggers at EOD close. Enter at NEXT day's open (same operational pattern as research/17).

sections.title: Universe selection â€” quality filters before backtest

items.label: Step 1 â€” base universe
items.body: All daily-bar symbols in market_data_unified (~1,623). Filter to stocks with >=1500 daily bars since 2018-01-01. Exclude Nifty 500 (already covered by research/17) â€” focus on small/micro-cap exposure.

items.label: Step 2 â€” minimum market cap proxy via turnover
items.body: Min avg daily turnover Rs 5 Cr over trailing 60 days (liquidity floor for actual execution). Below this, even small position sizes face 0.5%+ impact cost.

items.label: Step 3 â€” maximum turnover cap (keep it small/micro)
items.body: Max avg daily turnover Rs 100 Cr over trailing 60 days â€” ensures we're not just re-testing mid-caps already in research/17 universe.

items.label: Step 4 â€” circuit / freeze filter (red flag check)
items.body: Exclude stocks where >5% of last 60 trading days hit a daily move >=10% (proxy for circuit hits / news-driven manipulation / illiquid pricing). These stocks have execution risk: positions get stuck when circuit triggers.

items.label: Step 5 â€” series filter
items.body: Keep only stocks in the 'EQ' (regular cash) series. Exclude T2T (trade-to-trade), Z series, BE series â€” these have settlement constraints incompatible with a swing entry/exit pattern.

items.label: Step 6 â€” volatility floor
items.body: Min avg ATR-as-pct-of-price >= 1% (exclude completely range-bound dead stocks).

items.label: Stop loss
items.body: max(entry - 2 * ATR(14), entry * 0.92) â€” whichever closer to entry. Caps day-1 risk at 8%.

items.label: Target â€” fixed 25%
items.body: Entry Ã— 1.25. No trailing stop, no time stop except 60-day max-hold safety. Same logic as research/17 D1_fixed_25_8 (the OOS-validated winner).

items.label: Why fixed 25% (not trailing)
items.body: Research/17 walk-forward proved fixed targets dominate trailing stops on daily-bar momentum: 41-day avg hold lets winners run to full 25%, trail-based exits get whipped on retracements before the move completes.

items.label: Risk per trade
items.body: 1% of equity. Quantity = floor((equity * 0.01) / (entry - stop)).

items.label: Max concurrent positions
items.body: 10 (same as research/17). On Rs 10L capital, max Rs 1L notional per position.

items.label: Costs
items.body: 0.30% round-trip (vs research/17's 0.20% â€” small caps have wider spreads). Stress test at 0.50%.

items.label: Capital allocation
items.body: Rs 10,00,000 initial. Long-only (small-cap shorts via cash impossible; F&O futures unavailable on this universe). Skips short signal handling entirely.

sections.title: Backtest scope
sections.body: Period 2018-01-01 to 2025-12-31 (8 years). Bar TF: daily. Capital: Rs 10L. Walk-forward train 2018-2022 / test 2023-2025 (matches research/17 split for direct comparison).

sections.title: Pass criteria
items: OOS Profit Factor >= 1.20 (same as research/17)
OOS Sharpe >= 0.8
OOS MaxDD <= 30% (same as research/17 â€” small-cap MaxDD will likely be higher than mid-cap, but we accept that for the higher CAGR potential)
Trade frequency: 30-100 trades/year across the curated universe

sections.title: Why this should work (a-priori thesis)
sections.body: Research/17 proved daily ATH/52w breakouts + volume + regime filter is structurally edge-positive on Nifty 500. Small-caps amplify the move size when they break out (higher beta of single-stock momentum), but also amplify the noise (worse spreads, more news-driven false breakouts). The strict quality filters (turnover, circuit history, EQ series, volatility floor) should remove enough noise to leave the genuine edge. Net expectation: similar PF/Sharpe to research/17 (1.4 / 0.9), potentially higher CAGR, slightly higher MaxDD.

plans.title: ORB Directional Options â€” Nifty
plans.subtitle: 5-min directional options overlay on the existing ORB cash signal

sections.title: Goal
sections.body: When the ORB signal fires on any of the 15 cash stocks, mirror that directional view on NIFTY via options. Exploit the already-computed ORB conviction to size an options trade without taking separate directional risk on the index. Paper-only until backtest confirms edge.

sections.title: Entry â€” three variants to evaluate

items.label: Variant A Â· Pure naked option sell
items.body: On SHORT signal â†’ SELL ATM or slightly-OTM NIFTY CE. On LONG signal â†’ SELL ATM or slightly-OTM NIFTY PE.
pros: Collect full premium if direction is right
Simple 1-leg management
Maximum theta capture
cons: Unbounded risk on the wrong-side tail
Margin intensive

items.label: Variant B Â· Directional strangle
items.body: On SHORT signal â†’ SELL closer-ATM CE + SELL far-OTM PE. On LONG signal â†’ SELL closer-ATM PE + SELL far-OTM CE. Far-leg acts as tail hedge and improves margin.
pros: Covers both tails partially
Lower net margin via spread benefit
Extra premium from the hedge leg
cons: Two-leg management overhead
Caps upside vs naked

items.label: Variant C Â· Conviction-ranked router
items.body: Route grade A+/A signals to Variant A (naked â€” high conviction) Â· Route B/C to Variant B (hedged) Â· Skip anything with no stars.
pros: Uses existing conviction rubric already saved on positions
Right risk profile for each signal quality
cons: Adds complexity
Needs sample size to confirm grade-vs-outcome correlation

items.label: 1. Hard SL at 2Ã— entry premium
items.body: Classic short-premium rule. Max loss = initial premium collected. Place as exchange SL-L with trigger at 2Ã— entry, limit at 2.1Ã— entry.

items.label: 2. SuperTrend trail on NIFTY spot
items.body: Exit the option when NIFTY spot SuperTrend flips against the trade. Test ST(7, 2.0) and ST(7, 3.0) â€” narrower band (2.0) catches smaller reversals but may whipsaw.

items.label: 3. Time-based exit
items.body: Candidates: 14:30 (aggressive) Â· 15:15 (standard EOD) Â· 15:20 (Zerodha MIS auto-squareoff). Test whether 14:30 loses the late-afternoon intrinsic decay edge.

items.label: 4. Partial scale-out
items.body: Mirror the ORB R-multiple style: exit 50% of lots at +30% premium decay (i.e. option value drops to 0.7Ã— entry) Â· let remainder ride till SL or ST-flip.

sections.title: Open design questions
items: Strike selection: fixed-offset from spot? LTP-based search targeting premium ~Rs 80? Delta-based (e.g. short the 0.30 delta)?
Capital allocation: 1 lot per ORB signal? Scaled by conviction grade? Capped by current MIS margin headroom?
Concurrent cap: how many parallel option positions? 1? 3? 5?
Expiry choice: current-week for max theta + max gamma, or next-week for smoother decay?
SL placement: exchange-side SL-L on the option leg, or monitor-driven 2Ã— check every tick?
Direction conflict: what if two ORB stocks give opposite signals? Take both (opposing NIFTY options), net out, or pick by conviction?

sections.title: Prerequisites before backtesting
items: Need 20+ trading days of captured 1-min options data (currently 3 sessions)
Reconstruct 5-min NIFTY candles from 1-min data for the ST calculation
Wire the ORB signal bus to emit signals to a second consumer (options engine)
Build a simple backtester using the captured option_chain snapshots

sections.title: Integration notes
items: Reuses the existing ORB signal engine 1:1 â€” no new signal logic
Fires at the same moment the cash ORB trade fires (so same filters apply)
Conviction grade already saved on orb_positions â€” direct lookup
New DB: backtest_data/orb_options.db (positions / orders / pnl)
Keep in paper mode (nas-style) until backtest shows edge

plans.title: Intraday Final Live Setup â€” 3 Configs (Original 78% WR + Cost-Resilient + Multi-Bar Bounce)
plans.subtitle: LOCKED 2026-05-06 after research/37 â†’ research/38 â†’ cost-stress reality check. Three configs running in parallel: A (research/37 original 78% WR at TP 0.5/SL 1.5, cost-fragile), B (same signals retuned to TP 2.0/SL 1.5 for cost-resilience, 53% WR / RR 1.33 / +20% / Sharpe 2.38 at 0.10%/side), C (research/38 multi-bar SHORT bounce, walk-forward 59.6% WR / RR 1.5 / PF 1.69 / DD 3.1%). Paper-trade before scaling. Full spec: research/37/INTRADAY_FINAL_LIVE_SETUP.md

sections.title: ðŸ”’ LOCKED â€” three configs to deploy in parallel
sections.body: After research/37 found 78% WR but cost-fragile, then research/38 hunted favorable-RR alternatives, the final lock-in is THREE configs that deploy together. Position size: Rs.3K risk/trade (ORB convention). Max 5 concurrent positions across all configs combined. MIS 5x leverage. See full spec doc.

items.label: CONFIG A â€” 3-System Original (TP 0.5%/SL 1.5%) â€” research/37
items.body: All 3 systems (Diamond SHORT + Long-MR + Long-TC) on 67 stocks. TP 0.5% / SL 1.5% / EOD. WR 78.1% test. PF 1.28. MaxDD 4.5%. Sharpe 2.55. ~895 trades/yr. COST-FRAGILE: works at 0.05%/side; loses at 0.10%/side. Use only on top-volume liquid names with realistic 0.05%/side execution.
pros: Highest test WR (78.1%) â€” psychologically attractive
Heavy trade frequency (~895/yr) â€” capital deploys often
Lowest test MaxDD (4.5%)
cons: 1:3 RR â€” one SL eats 3 wins
Cost-fragile at >=0.10%/side (flips to negative)
Requires liquid-name execution to keep slippage <0.05%/side

items.label: CONFIG B â€” 3-System Cost-Resilient (TP 2.0%/SL 1.5%) â€” research/37 retuned
items.body: Same 3 signals as A, retuned exit to TP 2.0% / SL 1.5% / EOD (RR 1.33 favorable). WR 53.5% (random WR is 43%, +11pp real edge â€” same edge as A). PF 1.26. MaxDD 4.5%. Sharpe 2.38. +20% return at 0.10%/side. ~830 trades/yr.
pros: Cost-resilient â€” survives 0.10%/side slippage
Favorable RR (1.33:1)
Same Sharpe and DD as A
cons: Lower WR â€” psychologically harder
Wider TP means more EOD exits, longer position holds within session

items.label: CONFIG C â€” Multi-Bar SHORT Bounce â€” research/38 winner
items.body: 4 consecutive bearish 5-min bars + close<VWAP + RSI<=55 + NIFTY below own VWAP at scan. SHORT entry. TP 1.5% / SL 1.0% (RR 1.5). On the same 25 short-diamond cohort as A1. Walk-forward: train WR 56.7% PF 1.54 â†’ test WR 59.6% PF 1.69 (drift -3pp test BETTER), test DD 3.1%, n=57 over 6mo. ~120 trades/yr.
pros: Highest test PF (1.69) of all three configs
Lowest test MaxDD (3.1%)
Different signal mechanism â€” uncorrelated to A/B
cons: Lower frequency (~120/yr)
Short-only (no long complement at this RR)
Test n=57 needs paper-trade extension

sections.title: Concurrency rules + position sizing
items: Don't run A AND B on the same stock simultaneously â€” same signal, different exits â†’ conflict.
Recommended live deploy: A + C (max trade frequency) OR B + C (cost-resilient + favorable-RR diversifier).
Risk per trade: Rs.3,000 fixed (ORB convention) â†’ position size = floor(3000 / (entry_price Ã— sl_pct/100)).
Max 5 concurrent positions across all configs combined.
Daily loss circuit-breaker: 3 SL hits â†’ halt for the day.
MIS 5x leverage on Indian equity intraday â€” Rs.10L NAV supports up to Rs.50L gross notional.

sections.title: Why three, not one â€” mechanism breakdown
items: A rides per-stock structural drift (Stage 7 short-diamonds have 60-72% WR unconditionally) at low cost on liquid names. Highest WR but cost-fragile.
B uses the SAME signals as A but waits for bigger moves â€” fewer TP fires, more EOD exits, but each exit has realistic post-cost edge. Cost-resilient.
C is a different signal mechanism (multi-bar bearish continuation + NIFTY weakness gating). Fires less often (~5/stock/yr) but highest PF and lowest DD.
When markets are cleanly weak: A and C both fire. When markets are sloppy/trending: B picks up trades A misses.

sections.title: Trigger rules (deployable spec â€” same for A/B, different exit only)
items: A1+B1 â€” Diamond Short: 09:45 IST, stock RSI(14)<40 + close<VWAP + NIFTY first-30-min<0 â†’ SHORT at next bar's open
A2 â€” Long-MR: 11:15-13:15, stock down â‰¥-2% from open + RSI bounce 28â†’35 + bullish bar + NIFTY not crashing â†’ LONG at next bar's open
A3 â€” Long-TC: 09:15-10:30, stock gap-up â‰¥0.5% + first-hour break â‰¥0.5% + price within 0.3% of VWAP + RSIâ‰¥45 + NIFTY also gap-up bullish â†’ LONG at next bar's open
C â€” Multi-Bar SHORT Bounce: continuous scan, last 4 bars all bearish + lower-highs + close<VWAP + RSIâ‰¤55 + NIFTY<own-VWAP â†’ SHORT at next bar's open
Exit (A): TP 0.5% / SL 1.5% / EOD 15:25 IST | Exit (B): TP 2.0% / SL 1.5% / EOD | Exit (C): TP 1.5% / SL 1.0% / EOD

sections.title: Cohorts (refresh every 6 months)
items: A1+B1+C SHORT cohort (25 stocks): ZEEL, EDELWEISS, ASHOKA, CDSL, BANDHANBNK, KNRCON, RAIN, GMDCLTD, HEG, NATCOPHARM, SJVN, PRAJIND, TIINDIA, SUZLON, AMBER, RCF, NETWORK18, NAM-INDIA, BAYERCROP, TCIEXP, AARTIIND, NATIONALUM, IDEA, LTTS, NBCC
A2 Long-MR cohort (15 stocks): BALRAMCHIN, AUROPHARMA, DCBBANK, CYIENT, APLLTD, CERA, CGCL, BOSCHLTD, EIHOTEL, ASTRAL, CESC, CAPLIPOINT, AAVAS, ALKEM, APLAPOLLO

A3 Long-TC cohort (30 stocks): MARICO, FINEORG, CCL, ASAHIINDIA, GALAXYSURF, ASTRAZEN, JKPAPER, M&MFIN, JUBLFOOD, WHIRLPOOL, GODREJAGRO, INDIANB, ZYDUSWELL, CHOLAFIN, WELCORP, CUB, AMBER, INDIACEM, PNBHOUSING, HINDZINC, JKCEMENT, SOBHA, MGL, GRINDWELL, BAJFINANCE, DIXON, APLAPOLLO, DBL, BANDHANBNK, GODFRYPHLP
67 unique stocks across cohorts (3 shared: AMBER, BANDHANBNK, APLAPOLLO)

sections.title: Production checklist before going live
items: Re-confirm all three cohorts with 6-month rolling re-screen
Stress-test with 0.15%/side cost â€” if execution doesn't beat 0.07%/side, default to Config B not A
Verify SLB/MIS borrow availability for short-cohort daily
Codify NIFTY filter as session-level gate (per-bar for A2/A3/C; once-at-09:45 for A1/B1)
Daily loss circuit-breaker at 3 SLs combined
Paper-trade for 2-3 weeks across all configs in mutually-respecting concurrency before scaling capital
Reduced-capital first deploy: Rs.2L â†’ Rs.5L â†’ Rs.10L over a month if paper holds

sections.title: Why the 75% WR + favorable-RR target was retired
sections.body: research/38 hunted hard for WR â‰¥ 75% with TP > SL across 7 patterns Ã— 310 stocks Ã— 1380+ cells. Result: empirically not achievable on intraday 5-min equity. Mathematical reason: random-walk WR with TP=X SL=Y is Y/(X+Y) â€” original 78% WR was largely a TP/SL artifact (random WR with 0.5/1.5 = 75%, real edge only +3pp). Reversing to favorable RR exposes the small edge. Best favorable-RR found: 60% WR. Pursued in research/39 (carry-forward) instead.
